trajectory.description: Observation: Starting first iteration. Need to establish a baseline and measure iteration time. Analysis: A standard ResNet50 pre-trained on ImageNet, fine-tuned for 5 epochs, will serve as a good baseline. Resizing 32x32 CIFAR images to 224x224 allows us to leverage the pre-trained weights effectively. Decision: Write train.py and predict.py, train for 5 epochs, and submit for evaluation to get baseline metrics and timing.

trajectory.description: Observation: ResNet50 baseline achieved 0.5319 balanced accuracy, with severe degradation on few-shot classes (0.1284). Analysis: A stronger pre-trained model like CLIP ViT-B/16 has better representations and might generalize better to few-shot classes even without explicit re-weighting. Decision: Switch to CLIP ViT-B/16, adjust normalization, reduce batch size to 64 to prevent OOM, and train for 10 epochs to establish a new baseline.

[eval_harness] Phase 2: Running evaluator
[eval_harness] Result: {'balanced_accuracy': 0.4012, 'accuracy': 0.4012, 'many_shot_acc': 0.6465, 'medium_shot_acc': 0.4106, 'few_shot_acc': 0.1216}

trajectory.description: Observation: The CLIP ViT-B/16 model achieved 0.5628 balanced accuracy, but few-shot accuracy is still very low (0.2742) compared to many-shot (0.8185). Analysis: The dataset is highly imbalanced (long-tailed). To improve few-shot performance, we need to address this imbalance. We can use class-balanced loss re-weighting (inverse square root of class frequencies) and add data augmentation (RandAugment) to prevent overfitting on minority classes. Decision: Implement class weights in CrossEntropyLoss, add RandAugment, use CosineAnnealingLR, and train for 15 epochs. We will also save the best model based on validation accuracy.

[eval_harness] Phase 2: Running evaluator
[eval_harness] Result: {'balanced_accuracy': 0.5743, 'accuracy': 0.5743, 'many_shot_acc': 0.7571, 'medium_shot_acc': 0.6069, 'few_shot_acc': 0.3371}

trajectory.description: Observation: Class weights + RandAugment caused a massive drop in accuracy (0.5628 -> 0.4012), likely due to underfitting from strong augmentation and instability from aggressive loss weighting. Analysis: Logit Adjustment (Menon et al., 2021) is a more elegant and stable method for long-tailed recognition that modifies logits based on class frequencies without changing the loss landscape as drastically. I will also replace RandAugment with standard CIFAR augmentations (RandomCrop, RandomHorizontalFlip) to prevent underfitting. Decision: Implement Logit Adjustment during training, use standard augmentations, and train for 12 epochs.

trajectory.description: Observation: Logit adjustment improved balanced accuracy to 0.5743, but few-shot accuracy is still lagging (0.3371). Analysis: We can further improve generalization and few-shot performance by training longer (25 epochs), using a better learning rate schedule (OneCycleLR), adding Mixup augmentation, using label smoothing, and slightly increasing the logit adjustment temperature (tau=1.2). Decision: Update train.py with Mixup, Label Smoothing, OneCycleLR, and tau=1.2. Train for 25 epochs.

[eval_harness] Result: {'balanced_accuracy': 0.6945, 'accuracy': 0.6945, 'many_shot_acc': 0.7771, 'medium_shot_acc': 0.6869, 'few_shot_acc': 0.6126}

trajectory.description: Observation: Logit adjustment with tau=1.2, mixup, and OneCycleLR improved balanced accuracy to 0.705. Few-shot accuracy improved significantly to 0.5639. Analysis: The model is learning well, but we can push regularization and training duration further to close the gap between many-shot and few-shot performance. CutMix is known to be very effective for CIFAR datasets. We will replace our custom mixup with timm's robust Mixup/CutMix implementation, add stochastic depth (drop_path_rate=0.1) to the ViT model, increase tau slightly to 1.3, and train for 40 epochs. Decision: Update train.py and predict.py to include these changes, train for 40 epochs, and evaluate.

[eval_harness] Result: {'balanced_accuracy': 0.6712, 'accuracy': 0.6712, 'many_shot_acc': 0.7921, 'medium_shot_acc': 0.6883, 'few_shot_acc': 0.5194}

trajectory.description: Observation: In iteration 6, balanced accuracy dropped slightly to 0.6945, but few-shot accuracy improved to 0.6126 while many-shot dropped to 0.7771. Analysis: The logit adjustment (tau=1.3) was too aggressive, over-penalizing head classes. Additionally, CutMix might be too destructive for 32x32 images resized to 224x224. Decision: Reduce tau to 1.15, disable CutMix (keep Mixup), add Model Exponential Moving Average (EMA) for better generalization, add Test-Time Augmentation (TTA) in predict.py, and train for 30 epochs.
